crime: The girl was her most faithful audience, and never once looked past the jokes to the one who made them. The girl laughed at every joke she made; the one evening she made none, the girl rose and crossed to a livelier table without a word.
That morning she fed the loudest girl a line, the one sure to land hardest at the evening games — a laugh her own words raised was hers, in whatever mouth it was told.
That evening the girls clustered at the head of the great stairs, and the planted line landed. The whole cluster broke up laughing.
In the laugh's few seconds, unwatched, she crossed and put the girl down the great stairs.
The laugh died as the girls turned to the body at the foot of the stairs. Every one of them would swear no one had moved.
But one girl had been streaming on her phone. A recording sees her plainly, and the stream held her crossing to the girl in the very seconds no eye could follow.
And the peak laugh had been one her own planted line raised. A lifetime of clowning had only ever been the making of just such blind moments.
fields.epitaph: Every laugh she raised was a place to vanish into; the night none came, they looked, and there was only her.
execution: The stand rose bare and bright, and she stood alone on it; a warden called the house to silence, and every gaze fixed on her.
A clear glass hood lowered over her and sealed.
She played to the house to the last, and no laugh answered; every eye stayed on her.
The air ran out inside the sealed hood.

magic.name: Laughblind
magic.text: she plants a line, and the laugh it draws is hers, in whatever mouth it's told; while that laugh lasts, no eye can settle on her.
